summary: Small item decomp matching PR using permuter-found source-shape edits across Link boomerang, Link hookshot, and Ness yo-yo item TUs. The bot report says five item functions became newly matched, improving matched code by 2644 bytes.

key_files.role: Adjusted local JObj assignment shape in it_802A0534, reported as newly 100% matched.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: it_802A0534 changes HSD_JObj* jobj into jobj_tmp then jobj
text_corpus: bot report lists itlinkboomerang it_802A0534 +5 bytes, 99.31% to 100.00%

key_files.role: Main matching target; changed inline helpers, declaration/order/lifetime shape, bool temporary, PAD_STACK removal, and argument/access expression choices across fn_802A33A0, it_802A39FC, and it_802A4758.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_802A33A0_GetFighter inline added with // permuterslop comment
raw_diff: fn_802A33A0 changes fp source to helper and it_802A5E28 fourth argument from fp->x40 to attr->x40
raw_diff: it_802A39FC initializes item and attr at declarations, changes int var_r0 to bool chk, removes PAD_STACK(4), adds y_delta nested temporary
raw_diff: it_802A4758_permuterslop inline returns &link_0->pos and is passed to test_comp
text_corpus: bot report lists fn_802A33A0, it_802A39FC, and it_802A4758 as newly 100% matched

key_files.role: Extracted rotation update into a static inline helper and adjusted PAD_STACK placement/size for itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Anim, reported as newly 100% matched.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Anim_UpdateRotation inline extracted, PAD_STACK changed from 24 to 16 in helper and PAD_STACK(16) added in caller
text_corpus: bot report lists itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Anim +2 bytes, 99.58% to 100.00%

what_changed.text: PR body states that a new permuter yielded several wins and records remaining item work as 21 item TUs and 69 item functions.
Five functions were reported as newly matched by the bot: it_802A4758, it_802A0534, fn_802A33A0, it_802A39FC, and itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Anim.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report for GALE01 lists 5 new matches

what_changed.text: itlinkhookshot.c added small inline wrappers/accessors and reshaped locals/control temporaries in several functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itlinkhookshot.c hunks for fn_802A33A0, it_802A39FC, it_802A4758

what_changed.text: itnessyoyo.c moved part of itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Anim into a static inline UpdateRotation helper and adjusted stack padding.
itlinkboomerang.c introduced an extra temporary JObj pointer assignment in it_802A0534.

smart_moves.text: Kept permuter-found changes narrow: only three item source files changed, and the bot report ties the edits to five newly matched functions.
Used small inline extraction/accessor wrappers to influence codegen without broad rewrites.
Converted an integer collision-check flag in it_802A39FC into a bool variable named chk while preserving the two mpCheckAllRemap branches.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: it_802A39FC var_r0 to chk changes

decomp_lessons.text: Very small source-shape changes, including extra pointer temporaries, declaration ordering, helper extraction, and local lifetime changes, can close final sub-percent gaps in item functions when backed by bot/objdiff-style match evidence.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists five functions moving to 100.00%

decomp_lessons.text: Permuter-found helper wrappers should be treated as matching tactics rather than general style; this PR even labels two helpers as permuter-driven/permuterslop.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: New permuter yielded a few wins

naming_conventions.text: Temporary or tactic-oriented helper names in this PR use explicit suffixes/comments such as GetFighter, UpdateRotation, and permuterslop to describe the local operation or flag the codegen tactic.
naming_conventions.notes: The PR evidence shows these names were merged, but it does not prove they are preferred global naming style.

naming_conventions.text: The it_802A39FC flag local was renamed from generated-looking var_r0 to role-adjacent chk.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: int var_r0 changed to bool chk
naming_conventions.notes: The name is short and local; no broader convention is implied.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Introduce an extra temporary pointer assignment to affect register/lifetime shape: HSD_JObj* jobj_tmp = gobj->hsd_obj; HSD_JObj* jobj = jobj_tmp.
Use a static inline accessor wrapper for owner fighter retrieval in fn_802A33A0.
Adjust declaration order, initialization-at-declaration, stack padding, and nested temporary lifetime to match it_802A39FC.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: it_802A39FC local declarations and PAD_STACK removal

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Wrap &link_0->pos in a static inline function before passing it to test_comp.
Extract a rotation update block into a static inline helper and split PAD_STACK usage between helper and caller.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: GALE01 report showed matched code 70.15% (+0.07%, +2644 bytes) and 5 new matches.
No review comments or review records are present in the slice.

notes: Changed files, exact diffs, PR metadata, and bot match report are available and consistent.
raw_pr_json is truncated, but the loaded excerpts include the needed PR identity and body.
No human review comments are present, so reviewer rationale and cleanup expectations cannot be inferred.
The bot report supports newly matched functions and byte progress; the slice does not include full objdiff output or build logs.
The term permuterslop appears in source diff, but no evidence says whether maintainers considered it a long-term style or only an expedient matching tactic.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Permuter-found micro-shape edits closed five item matches in PR 2579
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2579, narrow source-shape edits across itlinkhookshot.c, itlinkboomerang.c, and itnessyoyo.c corresponded to a bot report of five newly 100% matched item functions and +2644 matched bytes.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: New permuter yielded a few wins
text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report lists 5 new matches

accepted_candidate_records.title: Inline helper extraction and local lifetime reshaping used as final-gap tactics
accepted_candidate_records.text: This PR used small static inline wrappers, declaration ordering, temporary variable blocks, bool flag reshaping, and PAD_STACK changes to move specific item functions to 100%. Treat as tactic evidence for these functions, not as a broad style rule.

source_update_candidates.title: Clarify handling of permuter-generated helper wrappers
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding a note that permuter-found static inline wrappers, extra temporaries, and PAD_STACK/local-lifetime changes may be valid narrow matching tactics when accompanied by function-level match evidence, but names/comments like permuterslop should prompt cleanup review before treating the pattern as source style.
evidence_refs: PR 2579 title/body: permuter-driven fixes; New permuter yielded a few wins
text_corpus: bot report confirms affected functions reached 100.00%
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR provides concrete examples of permuter-driven helper/tactical source shapes that matched functions, while the naming/comment evidence suggests they should not automatically become accepted authored style.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all permuter output is accepted style; the PR only shows narrow merged changes with automated match evidence.
Do not infer human reviewer intent or cleanup approval because the slice has no human review comments.
Do not claim full TU completion; the PR body says remaining item TUs and functions still existed.
Do not promote attr->x40 versus fp->x40 as a semantic correction without additional type/source evidence; the diff only proves the PR changed the expression and the bot reported a match.
